channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Amnesty International says genocide is occurring in Gaza, an accusation Israel rejects
item.description: Amnesty International accuses Israel of committing genocide in the Gaza Strip during its war with Hamas, saying it has sought to deliberately destroy Palestinians by mounting deadly attacks, demolishing vital infrastructure and preventing the delivery of humanitarian aid.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Voters want more US involvement on world stage despite isolationist talk, Ronald Reagan Institute survey finds
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Institute released its sixth annual National Defense Survey and found 'record high' support for U.S. involvement abroad despite a growing sense that isolationism could be on the rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French lawmakers vote to oust prime minister in the first successful no-confidence vote since 1962
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s far-right and left-wing lawmakers joined together Wednesday in a historic no-confidence vote prompted by budget disputes that forces Prime Minister Michel Barnier and his Cabinet members to resign, a first since 1962. The National Assembly approved the motion by 331 votes.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: French government collapses after no-confidence vote passes against Prime Minister Michel Barnier
item.description: Prime Minister Michel Barnier is expected to resign after the National Assembly passed a vote of no-confidence on Wednesday, effectively collapsing the current government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A major power plant fails in Cuba, plunging the island into darkness — again
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — A new, widespread power outage plunged Cuba into darkness on Wednesday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, leaving millions without electricity and forcing authorities to suspend classes and work activities indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UK killer nurse Letby questioned over further baby deaths
item.description: Lucy Letby, a former British nurse who is serving a life sentence for the murders of babies in her care, has reportedly been questioned about more infant deaths.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump appoints former Middle East negotiator as hostage affairs envoy
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced his selection of Adam Boehler to serve as special presidential envoy for hostage affairs in his second term. While the position holds the rank of an ambassador, it does not require Senate confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: French lawmakers vote to oust prime minister in the first successful no-confidence vote since 1962
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s far-right and left-wing lawmakers joined together to vote Wednesday a no-confidence motion prompted by budget disputes that forces Prime Minister Michel Barnier to resign. The National Assembly approved the motion by 331 votes. A minimum of 288 were needed.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria's insurgency leader tours seized city of Aleppo, as fierce battles intensify near Hama
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The leader of Syria's most powerful insurgent group toured the seized city of Aleppo on Wednesday in a surprise visit — the first since the group captured large parts of the city over the weekend as fierce fighting intensifies in the government-led counter-offensive in northern Hama.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korean President Yoon's martial law declaration raises questions over his political future
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning martial law declaration lasted just hours, but experts say it raised serious questions about his ability to govern for the remaining 2 1/2 years of his term and whether he will abide by democratic principles.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's FBI pick Kash Patel targeted in Iranian cyberattack
item.description: Kash Patel, President-elect Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, was among those targeted by an Iranian cyber attack. It’s unclear whether any information was gained through the attack, but it makes Patel the latest figure in the Trump campaign to be pursued by the nation.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Iranian activist, Nobel prize winner Mohammadi released from jail for medical treatment
item.description: Human rights activist Narges Mohammadi, who is imprisoned in Iran on charges including spreading propaganda, had her jail term suspended to undergo medical treatment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hispanic senators press Biden to protect immigrants before leaving White House
item.description: The Senate’s three current Hispanic Democrats are calling on President Biden to extend and strengthen humanitarian protections for certain groups of immigrants whose legal status could be stripped by the incoming Trump administration. In a letter on Wednesday, Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto (Nev.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Archaeologists in Denmark discover over 100 weapons from the Iron Age during excavations
item.description: Archaeologists in Denmark unearthed what they've suggested is a sacrifice of weapons. The weapons likely belonged to a powerful chieftain thousands of years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New UN aid chief vows 'ruthlessness' to prioritize spending as funding for world's crises shrinks
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The new head of the U.N. humanitarian aid agency says it will be “ruthless” when prioritizing how to spend money, a nod to challenges in fundraising for civilians in war zones like Gaza, Sudan, Syria and Ukraine. Tom Fletcher, a longtime British diplomat who took up the U.N.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says 'Africa is the future' as he pledges millions more on the last day of Angola visit
item.description: President Joe Biden has pledged another $600 million for an ambitious multi-country rail project in Africa as one of the final foreign policy moves of his administration.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Hungary’s effort to end Holocaust survivor lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday probed the international consequences of allowing a group of Holocaust survivors and their heirs to leverage U.S. courts to seek compensation from Hungary for confiscating their property.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: NATO chief wants less talk of peace process in Ukraine, more arms
item.description: NATO’s Secretary-General Mark Rutte argued that Ukraine needs to be put in a “position of strength” instead of being pushed toward peace talks, while speaking at a Tuesday conference ahead of the organization’s Foreign Ministers meeting this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel warns it will go after Lebanon directly if cease-fire with Hezbollah collapses
item.description: Israel issued a warning at Lebanon that it will not long distinguish between Beirut and Hezbollah should the cease-fire between Jerusalem and the terrorist group collapse following an exchange of strikes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean parliament overrides president's martial law order
item.description: National Assembly Speaker Woo Won Shik declaring that lawmakers “will protect democracy with the people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian tourist dies after being swept away by massive wave while doing yoga in Thailand
item.description: Russian tourist Kamilla Beliatskaya died on Friday after she was swept away by a large wave while doing yoga on a rock in Koh Samui, Thailand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration to announce $725M weapons package to Ukraine months before Trump is sworn in
item.description: The Biden administration is announcing a $725 million weapons package for Ukraine less than two months before President-elect Donald Trump takes office, a U.S. official says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Guinea stadium stampede kills 56 following clashes at soccer match
item.description: Reports said security had tried to use tear gas to restore calm after the chaos that followed a disputed penalty call.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American-Israeli IDF platoon commander killed in battle, body held in Gaza, IDF says
item.description: A 21-year-old American-Israeli believed to have been held hostage since October 2023 was confirmed dead by the Israel Defense Forces on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Volkswagen workers hold strikes to push back against proposed pay cuts
item.description: Volkswagen workers launched rolling strikes at nine plants across Germany to underscore their resistance to pay cuts
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elton John says he has lost his eyesight, struggles to see new musical
item.description: Elton John says he struggled to watch his new musical because he has lost his eyesight after contracting an infection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden travels to Africa where policies were ‘over-promised and under-delivered,' amid massive China expansion
item.description: President Biden visits Angola this week and is spending billions to get better supplies of electric vehicle battery metals, but the admin may have already been beaten by China's investments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas releases new hostage video of American-Israeli Edan Alexander
item.description: Hamas released a propaganda video Saturday showing the 20-year-old Alexander alive, marking his first proof of life since being kidnapped.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: 2 children and a woman crushed to death outside Gaza bakery amid food shortage
item.description: Two children and a woman were crushed to death Friday as a crowd of Palestinians pushed to get bread at a bakery in the Gaza Strip amid a worsening food crisis in the war-ravaged territory, medical officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Customers at this Starbucks can sip coffee and observe a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Coffee drinkers can sip their beverages and view a quiet North Korean mountain village from a new Starbucks at a South Korean border observatory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: British lawmakers give initial approval to a bill to allow terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British lawmakers gave initial approval on Friday to a bill to help terminally ill adults end their lives in England and Wales, following an impassioned debate that saw people sharing personal stories of loss and suffering.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful Mexican cartel accused of forced labor, human trafficking
item.description: The Gulf Cartel is a long-standing, powerful drug trafficking organization that operates throughout Tamaulipas.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Biden readies $725 million arms aid package for Ukraine: Reuters
item.description: The formal notification to Congress of the weapons package could come as soon as Monday, one official said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation
